title: NASA Launches $30M Robotic Rescue to Save Sinking Swift Telescope
description: NASA is initiating a historic $30 million rescue operation to deploy a three-armed robotic satellite to catch and tow the sinking Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory before it burns up in Earth's atmosphere. Launch targets July 1 from Kwajalein Atoll.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: ISS Canadarm2 Wrist Joint Repair | Astronauts Complete 7-Hour Spacewalk
description: NASA astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir completed a seven-hour spacewalk on June 30 to replace a broken 200-pound wrist joint on the ISS Canadarm2 robotic arm. The repair restored full robotic arm operations just in time for Canada Day.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Inside NASA Cold Atom Lab | Freezing Atoms to Near Absolute Zero on the ISS
description: NASA upgraded the Cold Atom Lab on the ISS, cooling atoms to near absolute zero to create Bose-Einstein Condensates in microgravity. The Quantum 2.0 project enables deep space navigation and dark matter research.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Quantum Sensing Breakthrough Clears Path for Dark Matter and Gravitational Wave Detector
description: UK-led collaboration in Nature demonstrates quantum sensing noise cancellation for AION atom interferometer. Tabletop prototype at Imperial College London achieves Standard Quantum Limit sensitivity, targeting 1 Hz mid-band gravitational waves and ultralight dark matter detection.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Tropical Butterfly Lives 25 Times Longer Than Cousins | New Aging Blueprint Discovered
description: University of Bristol and Smithsonian study in Nature Communications reveals Heliconius butterflies live up to 348 days due to evolved genetic mechanisms and pollen diet. A Pullinator grip strength test showed zero physical decline in aged individuals, challenging standard senescence models.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: SpaceX Preps Secret Starfall Reentry Capsule | Orbital Manufacturing Demo Launch Tomorrow
description: SpaceX launches Starfall reentry capsule demo on Falcon 9 from Cape Canaveral. The flat cylindrical vehicle returns up to 2,200 pounds of cargo from orbit, targeting the orbital manufacturing market with a 29-flight veteran booster B1078.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: SpaceX Preps Sunrise Launch for Secretive Starfall Reentry Capsule Demo
description: SpaceX launches Starfall reentry capsule Tuesday June 23 at 6:43 a.m. EDT aboard Falcon 9 B1078 on its 29th flight. The flat cylindrical capsule targets 2,200 pounds of cargo return for the orbital manufacturing market.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope | Arrives in Florida for Launch Processing
description: NASA Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope arrives at Kennedy Space Center for 70-day pre-launch processing. Falcon Heavy launch targeting August 30, 2026, to Sun-Earth L2 with a 300-megapixel Wide Field Instrument.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Rocket Report | Rebuild Begins at Blue Origin Launch Complex 36 After New Glenn Explosion
description: Blue Origin begins round-the-clock rebuild of Launch Complex 36 after New Glenn static fire explosion. Pad reconstruction started June 16 with a custom vertical integration building system.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: A Salty Surprise | JWST Detects Vaporized Salt Clouds on the Pink Planet GJ 504b
description: JWST detected vaporized salt clouds on exoplanet GJ 504b, the famous Pink Planet. Sodium chloride and potassium chloride found in its atmosphere by NIRSpec. New exoplanet chemistry data.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Quantum Soup Captured | Physicists Find Entanglement in a Visible Strange Metal Crystal
description: Physicists detected macroscopic quantum entanglement inside a visible centimeter-sized strange metal crystal. The Nature Physics study proves quantum states can thrive in solid matter.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Persian Gulf of Space | NASA Study Maps Massive Fuel Resources on Saturn Moon Titan
description: NASA Goddard study maps vast hydrocarbon fuel reserves and water ice on Saturn moon Titan. Methane lakes, LNG, and rocket propellant potential make Titan a deep-space refueling hub.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Ultimate Tipping Point | Antarctica Offers 30-Year Warning Before Irreversible 4-Meter Sea Level Rise
description: Nature study reveals 30-year predictable window for Antarctic sea level rise. West Antarctic Ice Sheet collapse may trigger 4-meter rise with a threshold already breached.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Off-World Overhaul | Jeff Bezos Declares Orbital AI Data Centers Are a Certainty at VivaTech 2026
description: Jeff Bezos says orbital AI data centers are inevitable once launch costs drop. Blue Origin aims to build the space infrastructure road for space-based computing at VivaTech 2026.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Turning the Tables | Google Borrows Nvidia Financial Playbook to Break AI Chip Monopoly
description: Alphabet clones Nvidia financial playbook with $3.2 billion in guarantees, $35 billion private-credit platform, and circular financing to force TPU adoption and break Nvidia AI chip dominance.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Blue Origin | New Glenn, New Shepard & Space Infrastructure
description: Blue Origin news and analysis from OzoneNews. New Glenn orbital launches, New Shepard suborbital flights, BE-4 engine production, and Blue Moon lunar lander development.
author: OzoneNews Editorial Team

title: NASA | Missions, Discoveries & Space Exploration
description: NASA missions, discoveries, and space exploration coverage from OzoneNews. Artemis Moon program, Mars rovers, James Webb Space Telescope, and deep space science.
author: OzoneNews Editorial Team

title: SpaceX | Starship, Falcon 9 & Commercial Spaceflight
description: SpaceX news and analysis from OzoneNews. Starship test flights, Falcon 9 launch cadence, Starlink deployments, and the future of reusable rocketry.
author: OzoneNews Editorial Team

title: Earth's Fungi Network Spans 110 Quadrillion Kilometers
description: A global SPUN study maps Earth's mycorrhizal fungal network at 110 quadrillion kilometers, storing up to 4 billion tons of CO2 annually in soil.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Galaxy-Killing Wind | JWST Solves Why Early Galaxies Die Young
description: Galaxy-killing wind found by JWST is stripping CRISTAL-02, solving why massive early universe galaxies died within 50 million years.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Researchers Find Hidden Damage To Coral Reefs By Scuba Divers
description: A new study shows scuba divers cause frequent, mostly unnoticed reef damage. The findings challenge the idea that diving tourism is inherently sustainable.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Coral Reef | Why Heat Stress and Bleaching Are Reshaping Ocean Life
description: Coral reefs are under pressure from heat stress, acidification, and disease. Here is what 2026 bleaching data and recovery trends show.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Earth | Climate, Oceans, and Coral Systems
description: Earth coverage focused on coral reefs, ocean health, climate resilience, and the ecosystems that sustain life on our planet.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Spain Has 3 Solar Eclipses This Summer | Dates, Viewing Locations, and What to Expect
description: Spain sees three solar eclipses in summer 2026, including a total eclipse on August 12. Get the exact dates, paths, and viewing tips for each event.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Scientists Find Wind Blowing From Our Milky Way's Black Hole | Half-Century Search Ends
description: JWST has confirmed a molecular wind driven by Sagittarius A*, the Milky Way's black hole, ending a 50-year search and rewriting galactic feedback models.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: West Coast Sea Stars Resurgence | After Massive Die-Off, Populations Recover Unevenly
description: After sea star wasting disease killed billions of sea stars from 2013 to 2016, West Coast populations are partially rebounding. Here is the current ecological status.
author: Josh Donnelly

title: Blazar Talon 1.5X Anamorphic | World's First Full Frame Autofocus Cinema Lens Series
description: Blazar Talon 1.5X is the world's first full-frame autofocus anamorphic lens, delivering a 1.8x cinematic look at T2.1 in three focal lengths from $1,099.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Antares Mark-0 Goes Critical | First Advanced US Nuclear Reactor in 40 Years
description: Antares Nuclear's Mark-0 microreactor achieved zero-power criticality at INL on June 4, the first advanced non-LWR in the US in more than 40 years under the DOE Reactor Pilot Program.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: ISS Zvezda Air Leak | NASA Crew Shelters in Dragon as Russia Attempts Emergency Repairs
description: NASA directed five astronauts to shelter in Dragon on June 5 as Russian cosmonauts attempted emergency repairs on the Zvezda module's worsening PrK air leak.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: NASA Orders Astronauts to Take Shelter After New Leak Aboard the International Space Station
description: NASA ordered the ISS crew to shelter in Dragon on June 5 after new Zvezda leaks worsened. Partial repairs completed within 90 minutes; no emergency was declared.
author: Kaustubh Madiraju

title: Bumblebees Match Chimps in Classic Problem-Solving Test | New Study Rewrites Invertebrate Intelligence
description: A study in Science finds buff-tailed bumblebees spontaneously use objects as tools to reach food, matching the cognitive problem-solving of chimpanzees.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: NASA Declares MAVEN Mars Orbiter Dead | Fatal Spin Ends 11-Year Mission After Six Months of Silence
description: NASA declared MAVEN dead on June 4, 2026, after a spin failure on December 6, 2025 ended six months of silence and 11 years of Mars science.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Sun Fires Triple Solar Flares | Cannibal CME Pushes Northern Lights Deep Into the US Tonight
description: Three solar flares merge into a cannibal CME, pushing Northern Lights as far south as Illinois and Oregon tonight, June 4-5, 2026.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Milky Way Black Hole Caught Breathing | 50-Year Mystery of Sagittarius A* Wind Solved
description: Astronomers using ALMA and Chandra detected a hot continuous wind from Sagittarius A*, solving a 50-year mystery about the Milky Way's black hole.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Blackwell Commences Its Reign | NVIDIA GeForce RTX 50-Series Architecture, Specs, and Pricing
description: NVIDIA's Blackwell RTX 50-series launches with the RTX 5090 at $1,999, featuring 32GB GDDR7 on a 512-bit bus and 1,792 GB/s memory bandwidth.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: SpaceX's $75B Record IPO | Inside Elon Musk's Out-of-This-World Capital Requirements
description: SpaceX priced its historic IPO at $135 per share targeting $75B, valuing the company at $1.77T. Wall Street is split: Oppenheimer bull vs. Morningstar bear. Full breakdown inside.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: AI's Hidden Thirst | How Google Is Engineering Its Way Out of the Data Center Water Crisis
description: AI models consume up to 500ml of water per query for server cooling. Google's three-part fix: air cooling, closed-loop liquid systems, and municipal heat recovery. Full breakdown.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Google Breaks Ground in Sweden | The 9-Year Road to Horndal and What It Means for European AI Infrastructure
description: Google broke ground in Horndal, Sweden on June 2, 2026. The 700MW facility closes a 9-year development and reshapes European AI infrastructure.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: NASA Artemis 3 | x
description: NASA locks Artemis III into late 2027 as a 463 km LEO crewed demonstration with SpaceX Starship HLS and Blue Moon Mark 1. Lunar landing moves to Artemis IV in 2028.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Starship Flight 10 | Engineering Legacy and the V3 Transition
description: SpaceX Starship Flight 10 with Ship 37 and Booster 16 exposed structural limits that directly shaped V3 megarocket design. Full engineering breakdown.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Blue Origin New Glenn Explosion | Launchpad Destruction Threatens Amazon Kuiper and NASA Lunar Architecture
description: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a static fire test at Cape Canaveral LC-36, destroying the pad and delaying Amazon Kuiper and NASA lunar missions.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Nvidia GeForce RTX 5070 Review | The Midrange Blackwell Dilemma in 2026
description: RTX 5070 review: GB205 Blackwell architecture, 12GB GDDR7, DLSS 4 Multi-Frame Generation, and 988 TOPS FP4 compute tested at 1440p and 4K in 2026.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Keeling Curve 2026 | CO2 Hits 433.95 ppm at Mauna Loa Peak
description: Mauna Loa Observatory recorded 433.95 ppm CO2 on May 1, 2026, the highest daily reading in the 68-year Keeling Curve record. NOAA monthly average pending June.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Pigeon Navigation | Magnetic Compass Found in Liver, Not Brain
description: Pigeon magnetic navigation relies on iron-rich liver macrophages, not the brain. A Science study from University of Bonn rewrites how birds find their way home.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Arctic Sea Ice Minimum 2026 | NSIDC Measured Extent and Trend Data
description: The 2025 Arctic sea ice minimum was 4.28 million km², third-lowest on record. NSIDC data shows the long-term trend at minus 13% per decade. 2026 melt tracking has begun.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Great Barrier Reef Bleaching 2026 | AIMS Survey Data and Coral Status
description: AIMS 2026 monitoring data: 73% of surveyed reefs showed bleaching in the fifth mass event in eight years. Northern reef had record coral cover before the thermal stress event.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: NASA Artemis III Mission Status | Launch Window and Crew Update 2026
description: NASA delayed Artemis III to no earlier than mid-2027. SpaceX HLS needs a second demo flight and Axiom Space xEMU suits are in qualification testing at Johnson Space Center.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Ozone Layer Recovery Data 2026 | WMO Report Confirms Progress
description: The 2026 WMO ozone assessment confirms recovery to 1980 levels by 2045 at mid-latitudes and 2066 at the poles. Antarctic hole was fifth-smallest on record in 2025.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: SpaceX Starship Orbital Flight Record | Reuse Milestones and 2026 Manifest
description: SpaceX Starship Flight 9 completed May 27, 2026 with the second consecutive booster catch. Nine total flights since April 2023. NASA Artemis HLS certification requirements and what comes next.
author: Simon Alfred Minter
